verified.name: Fervo Energy
verified.description: Geothermal energy company based in Houston, Texas that generates electricity through enhanced geothermal systems (EGS) using horizontal drilling techniques adapted from oil and gas industry.
verified.notes: Company announced IPO filing on April 17, 2026 and launched IPO on May 4, 2026. Most recent funding was Series E of $462 million in November 2025 led by B Capital. Wikipedia source notes article quality concerns but founder names and founding year are consistent across sources.

changesProposed.name: Nuro

verified.name: Nuro
verified.description: Autonomous vehicle technology company that develops Level 4 driving systems. Founded in 2016, it initially built custom autonomous delivery vehicles and has pivoted since September 2024 to licensing its Nuro Driver proprietary technology to automakers and mobility providers.
verified.notes: Company pivoted from autonomous delivery vehicles to technology licensing model in September 2024. Series E funding opened April 2025 ($106M) and closed August 2025 ($203M additional at $6B valuation). Partnership with Uber and Lucid Motors to deploy 20,000+ robotaxis announced July 2025.

changesProposed.name: OpenAI

current.name: OpenAI
current.founder: Sam Altman, Greg Brockman, Ilya Sutskever

verified.name: OpenAI
verified.description: An American artificial intelligence research organization that developed the GPT family of large language models, DALL-E series of text-to-image models, and released ChatGPT in November 2022. OpenAI consists of a for-profit public benefit corporation partially controlled by a nonprofit foundation.
verified.notes: Wikipedia source [2] specifies October 2025 $6.6 billion share sale valuing company at $500 billion. Founded as nonprofit in December 2015; transitioned to for-profit capped entity in 2019; restructured in 2025 with nonprofit retaining 26% ownership. Sam Altman temporarily removed as CEO in November 2023 but reinstated five days later.

changesProposed.name: Redwood Materials

verified.name: Redwood Materials
verified.description: American battery-recycling company that recycles lithium-ion batteries to recover lithium, nickel, cobalt, and copper, and produces battery materials for electromobility and electrical storage systems. The company also deploys large-scale energy storage systems for data centers and the grid.
verified.notes: Wikipedia states $775M Series C (2021) and $350M Series E (2025), totaling $1.125B+ in disclosed funding. Valuation of $6 billion as of October 2025 per Wikipedia. Current stage not explicitly stated in sources. Company acquired Redux (German recycler) in 2023.

changesProposed.name: Space Forge

verified.name: Space Forge
verified.description: British aerospace manufacturing company developing reusable on-orbit fabrication capabilities to produce semiconductors and alloys in microgravity using the ForgeStar platform.
verified.notes: Wikipedia indicates Series A funding of £22.6 million (approximately $22.6M USD equivalent as stated in database) was announced in July 2024 combining support from NATO Innovation Fund, World Fund, and British Business Bank. ForgeStar-0 launched January 2023 but failed to achieve orbit. ForgeStar-1 successfully launched June 2025.

changesProposed.name: SpaceX

verified.name: SpaceX
verified.description: American aerospace and artificial intelligence company that designs and manufactures rockets, spacecraft, and satellite constellations. Founded by Elon Musk in 2002, SpaceX conducts more orbital launches annually than any other launch provider and operates the Starlink satellite internet constellation.
verified.notes: Wikipedia states company is headquartered at Starbase in Starbase, Texas (not El Segundo where it was first founded in 2002). Wikipedia indicates SpaceX 'is not publicly traded but is expected to have an initial public offering (IPO) in 2026.' Valuation of $800 billion comes from 'a 2025 offer to buy internal shares.' Source 5 (SEC Form D) mentions a SpaceX Investors 5 LLC raised $9,725,064 in May 2026, but this appears to be an internal secondary offering, not primary fundraising. Total raised cannot be verified from these sources.

changesProposed.name: Starcloud

current.name: Starcloud
current.founder: Philip Johnston, Ezra Feilden, Adi Oltean

verified.name: Starcloud
verified.description: A United States-based company that designs, builds, and deploys data centers in space using proprietary technology. The company launched Starcloud-1 in November 2025 equipped with an Nvidia H100 GPU.
verified.notes: Company was originally founded as Lumen Orbit in January 2024 in El Segundo, California and rebranded to Starcloud in March 2025 after a legal challenge from Lumen Technologies. Series A announced March 30, 2026, making it the fastest Y Combinator company to reach unicorn status at 17 months after completing the program.
